1	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
2	from dataclasses import dataclass
3	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
4	
5	import numpy as np
6	
7	
8	@dataclass
9	class ChoicesDecision:
10	    decision: str
11	    meta_info: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
12	
13	
14	class ChoicesSamplingMethod(ABC):
15	
16	    @property
17	    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
18	        return False
19	
20	    @abstractmethod
21	    def __call__(
22	        self,
23	        *,
24	        choices: List[str],
25	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
26	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
27	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
28	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
29	    ) -> ChoicesDecision: ...
30	
31	
32	class TokenLengthNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
33	
34	    def __call__(
35	        self,
36	        *,
37	        choices: List[str],
38	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
39	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
40	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
41	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
42	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
43	        """Select the option with the highest token length normalized prompt logprob."""
44	        best_choice = choices[np.argmax(normalized_prompt_logprobs)]
45	        meta_info = {
46	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
47	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
48	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
49	        }
50	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
51	
52	
53	token_length_normalized = TokenLengthNormalized()
54	
55	
56	class GreedyTokenSelection(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
57	
58	    def __call__(
59	        self,
60	        *,
61	        choices: List[str],
62	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
63	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
64	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
65	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
66	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
67	        """Select the option based on greedy logprob selection. For overlapping options
68	        where one option is a subset of a longer option, extend the shorter option using
69	        its average logprob for comparison against the longer option."""
70	
71	        num_options = len(choices)
72	        max_tokens = max(len(option) for option in input_token_logprobs)
73	        logprob_matrix = self._build_logprob_matrix(
74	            input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options
75	        )
76	        remaining = self._greedy_selection(logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens)
77	
78	        best_choice = choices[remaining[0]]
79	        meta_info = {
80	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
81	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
82	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
83	            "greedy_logprob_matrix": logprob_matrix.tolist(),
84	        }
85	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
86	
87	    def _build_logprob_matrix(self, input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options):
88	        logprob_matrix = np.zeros((num_options, max_tokens))
89	        for i, option in enumerate(input_token_logprobs):
90	            actual_logprobs = [token[0] for token in option]
91	            avg_logprob = np.mean(actual_logprobs)
92	            logprob_matrix[i, : len(option)] = actual_logprobs
93	            if len(option) < max_tokens:
94	                logprob_matrix[i, len(option) :] = avg_logprob
95	        return logprob_matrix
96	
97	    def _greedy_selection(self, logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens):
98	        remaining = np.arange(num_options)
99	        for j in range(max_tokens):
100	            max_logprob = np.max(logprob_matrix[remaining, j])
101	            remaining = remaining[logprob_matrix[remaining, j] == max_logprob]
102	            if len(remaining) == 1:
103	                break
104	        return remaining
105	
106	
107	greedy_token_selection = GreedyTokenSelection()
108	
109	
110	class UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
111	
112	    @property
113	    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
114	        return True
115	
116	    def __call__(
117	        self,
118	        *,
119	        choices: List[str],
120	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
121	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
122	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
123	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
124	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
125	        """Select the option with the highest average token logprob once normalized by
126	        the unconditional token logprobs.
127	
128	        The first unconditional token logprob is assumed to be None. If so, it is
129	        replaced with 0 for the purposes of normalization."""
130	
131	        if unconditional_token_logprobs is None:
132	            raise ValueError(
133	                "Unconditional token logprobs are required for this method."
134	            )
135	
136	        normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs = self._normalize_logprobs(
137	            input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs
138	        )
139	
140	        best_choice = choices[np.argmax(normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs)]
141	        meta_info = {
142	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
143	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
144	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
145	            "unconditional_token_logprobs": unconditional_token_logprobs,
146	            "normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs": normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs,
147	        }
148	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
149	
150	    def _normalize_logprobs(self, input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs):
151	        normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs = []
152	        for inputs, unconditionals in zip(
153	            input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs
154	        ):
155	            inputs_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in inputs])
156	            unconditionals_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in unconditionals])
157	            unconditionals_logprobs[0] = unconditionals_logprobs[0] or 0
158	            normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs.append(
159	                float(np.mean(inputs_logprobs - unconditionals_logprobs))
160	            )
161	        return normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs
162	
163	
164	unconditional_likelihood_normalized = UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized()
165

42	def run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync):
43	    try:
44	        state.ret_value = program.func(state, *func_args, **func_kwargs)
45	    except Exception as e:
46	        raise e
47	    finally:
48	        state.stream_executor.end()
49	
50	    if sync:
51	        state.stream_executor.sync()
52	
53	    if global_config.verbosity >= 2:
54	        print(state.text())
55	
56	
57	def run_program(
58	    program,
59	    backend,
60	    func_args,
61	    func_kwargs,
62	    default_sampling_para,
63	    stream,
64	    sync=False,
65	    use_thread=True,
66	):
67	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
68	        backend = backend.endpoint
69	    assert backend is not None, "Please specify a backend"
70	    func_kwargs.update(program.bind_arguments)
71	    stream_executor = StreamExecutor(
72	        backend,
73	        func_kwargs,
74	        default_sampling_para,
75	        chat_template=None,
76	        stream=stream,
77	        num_api_spec_tokens=program.num_api_spec_tokens,
78	        use_thread=use_thread,
79	    )
80	    state = ProgramState(stream_executor)
81	
82	    if stream:
83	        t = threading.Thread(
84	            target=run_internal, args=(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
85	        )
86	        t.start()
87	        return state
88	    else:
89	        run_internal(state, program, func_args, func_kwargs, sync)
90	        return state
91	
92	
93	def run_program_batch(
94	    program,
95	    backend,
96	    batch_arguments,
97	    default_sampling_para,
98	    num_threads,
99	    progress_bar,
100	    generator_style=False,
101	):
102	    if hasattr(backend, "endpoint"):
103	        backend = backend.endpoint
104	
105	    # Pre-cache the common prefix for a batch. The prefix is extracted by tracing the program.
106	    if global_config.enable_precache_with_tracing and len(batch_arguments) > 1:
107	        cache_program(program, backend)
108	
109	    # Run all programs
110	    if num_threads == "auto":
111	        num_threads = max(96, multiprocessing.cpu_count() * 16)
112	    num_threads = min(num_threads, len(batch_arguments))
113	
114	    if generator_style:
115	        return _run_program_batch_generator(
116	            program,
117	            backend,
118	            batch_arguments,
119	            default_sampling_para,
120	            num_threads,
121	            progress_bar,
122	        )
123	
124	    # Original code path when generator_style=False
125	    if num_threads == 1:
126	        rets = []
127	        if progress_bar:
128	            for arguments in tqdm.tqdm(batch_arguments):
129	                rets.append(
130	                    run_program(
131	                        program,
132	                        backend,
133	                        (),
134	                        arguments,
135	                        default_sampling_para,
136	                        False,
137	                        True,
138	                    )
139	                )
140	        else:
141	            for arguments in batch_arguments:
142	                rets.append(
143	                    run_program(
144	                        program,
145	                        backend,
146	                        (),
147	                        arguments,
148	                        default_sampling_para,
149	                        False,
150	                        True,
151	                    )
152	                )
153	    else:
154	        if progress_bar:
155	            pbar = tqdm.tqdm(total=len(batch_arguments))
156	
157	        with ThreadPoolExecutor(num_threads) as executor:
158	            futures = []
159	            for arguments in batch_arguments:
160	                futures.append(
161	                    executor.submit(
162	                        run_program,
163	                        program,
164	                        backend,
165	                        (),
166	                        arguments,
167	                        default_sampling_para,
168	                        False,
169	                        True,
170	                    )
171	                )
172	                if progress_bar:
173	                    futures[-1].add_done_callback(lambda _: pbar.update())
174	
175	            rets = [f.result() for f in futures]
176	        rets[-1].sync()
177	
178	        if progress_bar:
179	            pbar.close()
180	
181	    return rets
182	
183	
184	def _run_program_batch_generator(
185	    program,
186	    backend,
187	    batch_arguments,
188	    default_sampling_para,
189	    num_threads,
190	    progress_bar,
191	):
192	    """Helper function that yields results one by one using chunking to avoid overwhelming ThreadPoolExecutor."""
193	    if num_threads == 1:
194	        iterator = tqdm.tqdm(batch_arguments) if progress_bar else batch_arguments
195	        for arguments in iterator:
196	            yield run_program(
197	                program,
198	                backend,
199	                (),
200	                arguments,

200	                arguments,
201	                default_sampling_para,
202	                False,
203	                True,
204	            )
205	    else:
206	        pbar = tqdm.tqdm(total=len(batch_arguments)) if progress_bar else None
207	
208	        # Process in chunks to avoid overwhelming ThreadPoolExecutor
209	        # Otherwise, ThreadPoolExecutor.submit will block after adding certain number of tasks
210	        # so we will never reach "yield" until all tasks are done
211	        chunk_size = 200
212	
213	        with ThreadPoolExecutor(num_threads) as executor:
214	            for chunk_start in range(0, len(batch_arguments), chunk_size):
215	                chunk_end = min(chunk_start + chunk_size, len(batch_arguments))
216	                chunk_futures = []
217	
218	                # Submit chunk of tasks
219	                for i in range(chunk_start, chunk_end):
220	                    future = executor.submit(
221	                        run_program,
222	                        program,
223	                        backend,
224	                        (),
225	                        batch_arguments[i],
226	                        default_sampling_para,
227	                        False,
228	                        True,
229	                    )
230	                    if pbar:
231	                        future.add_done_callback(lambda _: pbar.update())
232	                    chunk_futures.append(future)
233	
234	                # Yield results from this chunk as they complete
235	                for future in chunk_futures:
236	                    yield future.result()
237	
238	        if pbar:
239	            pbar.close()
240	
241	
242	def cache_program(program, backend):
243	    from sglang.lang.tracer import extract_prefix_by_tracing
244	
245	    prefix = extract_prefix_by_tracing(program, backend)
246	    if prefix and len(prefix) > 64:
247	        backend.cache_prefix(prefix)
248	
249	
250	class StreamExecutor:
251	    """A stream executor that executes SGL expressions in a background thread."""
252	
253	    def __init__(
254	        self,
255	        backend,
256	        arguments,
257	        default_sampling_para,
258	        chat_template,
259	        stream,
260	        num_api_spec_tokens=None,
261	        use_thread=True,
262	    ):
263	        from sglang.lang.backend.base_backend import BaseBackend
264	
265	        self.sid = uuid.uuid4().hex
266	        self.backend: BaseBackend = backend
267	        self.arguments: Dict[str, Any] = arguments
268	        self.default_sampling_para = default_sampling_para
269	        self.stream = stream
270	
271	        self.variables = {}  # Dict[name: str -> value: str]
272	        self.variable_event = {}  # Dict[name: str -> event: threading.Event]
273	        self.meta_info = {}  # Dict[name: str -> info: str]
274	        self.is_finished = False
275	        self.error_ = None
276	
277	        # For completion
278	        self.text_ = ""  # The full text
279	
280	        # For chat
281	        self.messages_ = []  # The messages in the OpenAI API format
282	        self.chat_template = chat_template or self.backend.get_chat_template()
283	        self.cur_role = None
284	        self.cur_role_begin_pos = None
285	
286	        # For vision
287	        self.images_ = []
288	        self.cur_images = []
289	
290	        # For fork/join
291	        self.fork_start_text_pos = None
292	
293	        # For speculative execution
294	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
295	        self.speculated_text = ""
296	
297	        # Worker thread
298	        self.use_thread = use_thread
299	        if self.use_thread:
300	            self.queue = queue.Queue()
301	
302	            def _run_worker_in_context():
303	                self._thread_worker_func()
304	
305	            self.worker = threading.Thread(
306	                target=contextvars.copy_context().run, args=(_run_worker_in_context,)
307	            )
308	            self.worker.start()
309	
310	        # For streaming
311	        if stream:
312	            self.stream_text_event = threading.Event()
313	            self.stream_var_event = {}
314	        else:
315	            self.stream_text_event = None
316	            self.stream_var_event = None
317	
318	    def submit(self, expr: SglExpr):
319	        self._init_var_event(expr)
320	
321	        if self.use_thread:
322	            self.queue.put(expr)
323	        else:
324	            self._execute(expr)
325	
326	    def sync(self):
327	        if self.use_thread:
328	            self.queue.join()
329	
330	    def get_var(self, name):
331	        if name in self.variable_event:
332	            self.variable_event[name].wait()
333	        return self.variables[name]
334	
335	    def set_var(self, name, value):
336	        self.variables[name] = value
337	
338	    def get_meta_info(self, name, timeout=None):
339	        if name in self.variable_event:
340	            got = self.variable_event[name].wait(timeout)
341	            if not got:
342	                raise TimeoutError(f"Timeout while waiting for event '{name}'")
343	        ret = self.meta_info.get(name, None)
344	        return ret
345	
346	    def fork(
347	        self,
348	        size: int = 1,
349	        position_ids_offset: Optional[List[int]] = None,
350	    ):
351	        if size > 1 and str(self.text_):
352	            self.submit(SglCommitLazy())
353	
354	        self.sync()
355	        size = int(size)
356	
357	        exes = [
358	            StreamExecutor(
359	                self.backend,
360	                self.arguments,
361	                self.default_sampling_para,
362	                self.chat_template,
363	                self.stream,
364	            )
365	            for _ in range(size)
366	        ]
367	        for i in range(size):
368	            exes[i].variables = dict(self.variables)
369	            exes[i].text_ = str(self.text_)
370	            exes[i].messages_ = list(self.messages_)
371	            exes[i].cur_role = self.cur_role
372	            exes[i].cur_role_begin_pos = self.cur_role_begin_pos
373	            exes[i].fork_start_text_pos = len(self.text_)
374	            exes[i].images_ = list(self.images_)
375	
376	            # TODO(ying): handle API speculative execution
377	
378	        return exes
379	
380	    def text(self):
381	        self.sync()
382	        return self.text_
383	
384	    def messages(self):
385	        self.sync()
386	        return self.messages_
387	
388	    def error(self):
389	        self.sync()
390	        return self.error_
391	
392	    def end(self):
393	        if self.use_thread:
394	            if self.worker.is_alive():
395	                self.queue.put(None)
396	        self.backend.end_program(self)
397	
398	    def _thread_worker_func(self):
399	        error = None
400	
401	        while True:
402	            expr = self.queue.get()
403	            if expr is None:
404	                self.queue.task_done()
405	                break
406	
407	            try:
408	                self._execute(expr)
409	            except Exception as e:
410	                warnings.warn(f"Error in stream_executor: {get_exception_traceback()}")
411	                error = e
412	                break
413	            self.queue.task_done()
414	            if self.stream_text_event:
415	                self.stream_text_event.set()
416	
417	        # Clean the queue and events
418	        if error is not None:
419	            try:
420	                while True:
421	                    self.queue.task_done()
422	                    self.queue.get_nowait()
423	            except queue.Empty:
424	                pass
425	            for name in self.variable_event:
426	                self.variable_event[name].set()
427	            if self.stream_var_event:
428	                for name in self.stream_var_event:
429	                    self.stream_var_event[name].set()
430	            self.error_ = error
431	
432	        if self.stream_text_event:
433	            self.stream_text_event.set()
434	
435	        self.is_finished = True
436	
437	    def _execute(self, other):
438	        if isinstance(other, str):
439	            other = SglConstantText(other)
440	
441	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
442	
443	        if isinstance(other, SglConstantText):
444	            self._execute_fill(other.value)
445	        elif isinstance(other, SglGen):
446	            self._execute_gen(other)
447	        elif isinstance(other, SglSelect):
448	            self._execute_select(other)
449	        elif isinstance(other, SglExprList):
450	            for x in other.expr_list:
451	                self._execute(x)
452	        elif isinstance(other, SglRoleBegin):
453	            self._execute_role_begin(other)
454	        elif isinstance(other, SglRoleEnd):
455	            self._execute_role_end(other)
456	        elif isinstance(other, SglImage):
457	            self._execute_image(other)
458	        elif isinstance(other, SglVideo):
459	            self._execute_video(other)
460	        elif isinstance(other, SglVariable):
461	            self._execute_variable(other)
462	        elif isinstance(other, SglVarScopeBegin):
463	            self._execute_var_scope_begin(other)
464	        elif isinstance(other, SglVarScopeEnd):
465	            self._execute_var_scope_end(other)
466	        elif isinstance(other, SglCommitLazy):
467	            self._execute_commit_lazy_operations(other)
468	        elif isinstance(other, SglConcateAndAppend):
469	            if (
470	                global_config.enable_parallel_encoding
471	                and self.backend.support_concate_and_append
472	            ):
473	                self._execute_concatenate_and_append_kv_cache(other)
474	            else:
475	                self._execute_concatenate_and_append_text(other)
476	        elif isinstance(other, SglSeparateReasoning):
477	            self._execute_separate_reasoning(other)
478	        else:
479	            raise ValueError(f"Unknown type: {type(other)}")
480	
481	    def _execute_fill(self, value: str, prefix=False):
482	        value = str(value)
483	
484	        if (
485	            self.cur_role == "assistant"
486	            and self.num_api_spec_tokens is not None
487	            and self.backend.is_chat_model
488	            and not prefix
489	        ):
490	            self.backend.spec_fill(value)
491	            return
492	
493	        if self.speculated_text.startswith(value):
494	            self.speculated_text = self.speculated_text[len(value) :]
495	        else:
496	            self.speculated_text = ""
497	
498	        self.text_ += value
499	
500	    def _execute_image(self, expr: SglImage):
501	        path = expr.path
502	
503	        base64_data = encode_image_base64(path)
504	
505	        self.images_.append((path, base64_data))
506	        self.cur_images.append((path, base64_data))
507	        self.text_ += self.chat_template.image_token
508	
509	    def _execute_video(self, expr: SglVideo):
510	        path = expr.path
511	        num_frames = expr.num_frames
512	
513	        base64_data = encode_video_base64(path, num_frames)
514	
515	        self.images_.append((path, base64_data))
516	        self.cur_images.append((path, base64_data))
517	        self.text_ += self.chat_template.image_token
518	
519	    def _spec_gen(self, sampling_params):
520	        stop = sampling_params.stop
521	        max_new_tokens = sampling_params.max_new_tokens
522	        meta_info = {}
523	
524	        def regen():
525	            nonlocal meta_info
526	
527	            sampling_params.max_new_tokens = max(
528	                sampling_params.max_new_tokens, self.num_api_spec_tokens
529	            )
530	            sampling_params.stop = None
531	            self.speculated_text, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
532	                self, sampling_params=sampling_params
533	            )
534	
535	        def find_stop():
536	            if isinstance(stop, str):
537	                return self.speculated_text.find(stop)
538	            elif isinstance(stop, (tuple, list)):
539	                pos = -1
540	                for stop_str in stop:
541	                    stop_pos = self.speculated_text.find(stop_str)
542	                    if stop_pos != -1 and (pos == -1 or stop_pos < pos):
543	                        pos = stop_pos
544	                return pos
545	            else:
546	                raise Exception("Wrong type of stop in sampling parameters.")
547	
548	        if stop is None:
549	            if len(self.speculated_text) < max_new_tokens:
550	                regen()
551	            comp = self.speculated_text[:max_new_tokens]
552	            self.speculated_text = self.speculated_text[max_new_tokens:]
553	        elif isinstance(stop, (str, list, tuple)):
554	            if self.speculated_text == "":
555	                regen()
556	            stop_pos = find_stop()
557	            if stop_pos == -1:
558	                stop_pos = min(
559	                    sampling_params.max_new_tokens,
560	                    len(self.speculated_text),
561	                )
562	            comp = self.speculated_text[:stop_pos]
563	            self.speculated_text = self.speculated_text[stop_pos:]
564	        else:
565	            raise ValueError("Wrong type of stop in sampling parameters.")
566	
567	        return comp, meta_info
568	
569	    def _execute_gen(self, expr: SglGen):
570	        sampling_params = self._resolve_sampling_params(expr.sampling_params)
571	        name = expr.name
572	        if not self.stream:
573	            if self.num_api_spec_tokens is None:
574	                comp, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
575	                    self,
576	                    sampling_params=sampling_params,
577	                )
578	
579	            else:
580	                if self.backend.is_chat_model:
581	                    # Speculative execution on models with only chat interface.
582	                    # Store the calls into a temporary list.
583	                    # They will be lazily executed later.
584	                    comp, meta_info = self.backend.generate(
585	                        self,
586	                        sampling_params=sampling_params,
587	                        spec_var_name=name,
588	                    )
589	                    return
590	
591	                else:  # Speculative execution on models with completion interface
592	                    comp, meta_info = self._spec_gen(sampling_params)
593	            if isinstance(comp, list):
594	                self.text_ += comp[0]
595	            else:
596	                assert isinstance(comp, str)
597	                self.text_ += comp
598	
599	            self.variables[name] = comp

600	            self.meta_info[name] = meta_info
601	            self.variable_event[name].set()
602	        else:
603	            assert (
604	                self.num_api_spec_tokens is None
605	            ), "stream is not supported with api speculative execution"
606	            generator = self.backend.generate_stream(
607	                self, sampling_params=sampling_params
608	            )
609	
610	            self.variables[name] = ""
611	            self.stream_var_event[name].set()
612	
613	            for comp, meta_info in generator:
614	                self.text_ += comp
615	                self.variables[name] += comp
616	                self.meta_info[name] = meta_info
617	                self.stream_var_event[name].set()
618	                self.stream_text_event.set()
619	
620	            self.variable_event[name].set()
621	            self.stream_var_event[name].set()
622	
623	    def _execute_select(self, expr: SglSelect):
624	        choices_decision = self.backend.select(
625	            self, expr.choices, expr.temperature, expr.choices_method
626	        )
627	        if expr.name is not None:
628	            name = expr.name
629	            self.variables[name] = choices_decision.decision
630	            self.meta_info[name] = choices_decision.meta_info
631	            self.variable_event[name].set()
632	            if self.stream_var_event:
633	                self.stream_var_event[name].set()
634	        self.text_ += choices_decision.decision
635	
636	    def _execute_variable(self, expr: SglVariable):
637	        src_executor = expr.source_stream_executor
638	        value = src_executor.get_var(expr.name)
639	        self._execute_fill(value)
640	
641	    def _execute_role_begin(self, expr: SglRoleBegin):
642	        assert self.cur_role is None, "Nested roles are not allowed."
643	
644	        if len(self.messages_) == 0 and expr.role != "system":
645	            # Insert the default system message
646	            default_system = self.chat_template.default_system_prompt
647	            if default_system:
648	                self._execute_role_begin(SglRoleBegin("system"))
649	                self._execute_fill(default_system)
650	                self._execute_role_end(SglRoleEnd("system"))
651	
652	        self.cur_role = expr.role
653	
654	        prefix, _ = self.chat_template.get_prefix_and_suffix(expr.role, self.messages_)
655	
656	        self._execute_fill(prefix, prefix=True)
657	        self.cur_role_begin_pos = len(self.text_)
658	
659	    def _execute_role_end(self, expr: SglRoleEnd):
660	        if (
661	            self.cur_role == "assistant"
662	            and self.num_api_spec_tokens is not None
663	            and self.backend.is_chat_model
664	        ):
665	            # Execute the stored lazy generation calls
666	            self.backend.role_end_generate(self)
667	        self.cur_role = None
668	
669	        new_text = self.text_[self.cur_role_begin_pos :].lstrip()
670	
671	        _, suffix = self.chat_template.get_prefix_and_suffix(expr.role, self.messages_)
672	        self._execute_fill(suffix)
673	
674	        if self.cur_images:
675	            # OpenAI vision API format
676	            last_msg = {
677	                "role": expr.role,
678	                "content": [{"type": "text", "text": new_text}],
679	            }
680	            for image_path, image_base64_data in self.cur_images:
681	                last_msg["content"].append(
682	                    {
683	                        "type": "image_url",
684	                        "image_url": {
685	                            "url": f"data:image/jpeg;base64,{image_base64_data}"
686	                        },
687	                    }
688	                )
689	            self.messages_.append(last_msg)
690	            self.cur_images = []
691	        else:
692	            # OpenAI chat API format
693	            self.messages_.append({"role": expr.role, "content": new_text})
694	
695	    def _execute_var_scope_begin(self, expr: SglVarScopeBegin):
696	        self.variables[expr.name] = int(len(self.text_))
697	
698	    def _execute_var_scope_end(self, expr: SglVarScopeEnd):
699	        self.variables[expr.name] = self.text_[self.variables[expr.name] :]
700	        self.variable_event[expr.name].set()
701	
702	    def _execute_commit_lazy_operations(self, expr: SglCommitLazy):
703	        self.backend.commit_lazy_operations(self)
704	
705	    def _execute_concatenate_and_append_text(self, expr: SglConcateAndAppend):
706	        new_text = ""
707	        for s in expr.states:
708	            exe = s.stream_executor
709	            exe.sync()
710	            new_text += exe.text_[exe.fork_start_text_pos :]
711	
712	        self._execute_fill(new_text)
713	
714	    def _execute_concatenate_and_append_kv_cache(self, expr: SglConcateAndAppend):
715	        self_len = len(self.text_)
716	
717	        for i, s in enumerate(expr.states):
718	            exe = s.stream_executor
719	            exe.submit(SglCommitLazy())
720	
721	        for i, s in enumerate(expr.states):
722	            exe = s.stream_executor
723	            exe.sync()
724	            assert exe.fork_start_text_pos == self_len
725	            self.text_ += exe.text_[exe.fork_start_text_pos :]
726	
727	        src_rids = [state.stream_executor.sid for state in expr.states]
728	        self.backend.concatenate_and_append(src_rids, self.sid)
729	
730	    def _execute_separate_reasoning(self, expr: SglSeparateReasoning):
731	        if self.stream:
732	            # separate reasoning for stream is not supported
733	            return
734	
735	        if (
736	            self.cur_role == "assistant"
737	            and self.num_api_spec_tokens is not None
738	            and self.backend.is_chat_model
739	        ):
740	            # Execute the stored lazy generation calls
741	            self.backend.role_end_generate(self)
742	
743	        from sglang.srt.parser.reasoning_parser import ReasoningParser
744	
745	        reasoning_parser = ReasoningParser(expr.model_type)
746	        other = expr.expr
747	        if not other:
748	            return
749	        elif isinstance(other, SglGen) or isinstance(other, SglSelect):
750	            cur_text = self.get_var(other.name)
751	            reasoning, normal_text = reasoning_parser.parse_non_stream(cur_text)
752	            reasoning_name = expr.process_name_for_reasoning(other.name)
753	            self.set_var(other.name, normal_text)
754	            self.set_var(reasoning_name, reasoning)
755	            # the variable is ready to be used
756	            self.variable_event[reasoning_name].set()
757	            self.text_ = self.text_[: self.cur_role_begin_pos] + normal_text
758	        elif isinstance(other, SglExprList):
759	            for x in other.expr_list:
760	                self._execute_separate_reasoning(
761	                    SglSeparateReasoning(expr.model_type, x)
762	                )
763	
764	    def _init_var_event(self, expr):
765	        if isinstance(
766	            expr, (SglGen, SglSelect, SglVarScopeBegin, SglSeparateReasoning)
767	        ):
768	            self.variable_event[expr.name] = threading.Event()
769	            if self.stream:
770	                self.stream_var_event[expr.name] = threading.Event()
771	        elif isinstance(expr, SglExprList):
772	            for e in expr.expr_list:
773	                self._init_var_event(e)
774	
775	    def _resolve_sampling_params(self, sampling_params):
776	        """
777	        Construct sampling param based on default + override values
778	
779	        The default values of sampling are populated in `default_sampling_para` via sgl.function.run(...sampling_args)
780	        , and `sampling_params` contains the override values from sgl.gen().
781	
782	        Here we use default_sampling_para as the base and override the values if they exist in `sampling_params`.
783	        It also extends the stop tokens based on the chat template.
784	        """
785	
786	        # deepcopy is required because the dict has lists inside
787	        clone = copy.deepcopy(self.default_sampling_para)
788	
789	        for item in [
790	            "max_new_tokens",
791	            "min_new_tokens",
792	            "n",
793	            "stop",
794	            "stop_token_ids",
795	            "stop_regex",
796	            "temperature",
797	            "top_p",
798	            "top_k",
799	            "min_p",
800	            "frequency_penalty",
801	            "presence_penalty",
802	            "ignore_eos",
803	            "return_logprob",
804	            "logprob_start_len",
805	            "top_logprobs_num",
806	            "return_text_in_logprobs",
807	            "dtype",
808	            "regex",
809	            "json_schema",
810	        ]:
811	            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
812	            if value is not None:
813	                setattr(clone, item, value)
814	
815	        if self.chat_template.stop_str:
816	            if clone.stop == ():
817	                clone.stop = []
818	            elif isinstance(clone.stop, str):
819	                clone.stop = [clone.stop]
820	            clone.stop += self.chat_template.stop_str
821	
822	        return clone
823	
824	    def __del__(self):
825	        self.end()

828	class ProgramState:
829	    """The state of an SGL program."""
830	
831	    def __init__(self, stream_executor: StreamExecutor):
832	        self.stream_executor = stream_executor
833	
834	    def _role_common(self, name: str, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
835	        if expr is not None:
836	            role_expr = SglExprList([SglRoleBegin(name), expr, SglRoleEnd(name)])
837	            self.stream_executor.submit(role_expr)
838	            return role_expr
839	        else:
840	
841	            @contextmanager
842	            def role_scope():
843	                self.stream_executor.submit(SglRoleBegin(name))
844	                yield
845	                self.stream_executor.submit(SglRoleEnd(name))
846	
847	            return role_scope()
848	
849	    def system(self, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
850	        return self._role_common("system", expr)
851	
852	    def user(self, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
853	        return self._role_common("user", expr)
854	
855	    def assistant(self, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):
856	        return self._role_common("assistant", expr)
857	
858	    @contextmanager
859	    def var_scope(self, name: str):
860	        self.stream_executor.submit(SglVarScopeBegin(name))
861	        yield
862	        self.stream_executor.submit(SglVarScopeEnd(name))
863	
864	    def fork(
865	        self,
866	        size: int = 1,
867	        position_ids_offset: Optional[List[int]] = None,
868	    ):
869	        stream_executors = self.stream_executor.fork(size, position_ids_offset)
870	        states = [ProgramState(x) for x in stream_executors]
871	        state_group = ProgramStateGroup(states, self)
872	        return state_group
873	
874	    @contextmanager
875	    def copy(self, position_ids_offset: Optional[List[int]] = None):
876	        state_group = self.fork(1, position_ids_offset)
877	        try:
878	            yield state_group[0]
879	        finally:
880	            state_group.join()
881	
882	    def text(self):
883	        return self.stream_executor.text()
884	
885	    def messages(self):
886	        return self.stream_executor.messages()
887	
888	    def sync(self):
889	        return self.stream_executor.sync()
890	
891	    def error(self):
892	        return self.stream_executor.error()
893	
894	    def text_iter(self, var_name: Optional[str] = None):
895	        if self.stream_executor.stream:
896	            prev = 0
897	            if var_name is None:
898	                event = self.stream_executor.stream_text_event
899	                while True:
900	                    event.wait()
901	                    event.clear()
902	                    out = str(self.stream_executor.text_[prev:])
903	                    prev += len(out)
904	                    if out:
905	                        yield out
906	                    if self.stream_executor.is_finished:
907	                        break
908	            else:
909	                event = None
910	                while not event:
911	                    if var_name in self.stream_executor.stream_var_event:
912	                        event = self.stream_executor.stream_var_event[var_name]
913	                    if self.stream_executor.is_finished:
914	                        yield ""
915	                        return
916	
917	                while True:
918	                    event.wait()
919	                    event.clear()
920	                    out = str(self.stream_executor.variables[var_name][prev:])
921	                    prev += len(out)
922	                    if out:
923	                        yield out
924	                    if self.stream_executor.variable_event[var_name].is_set():
925	                        break
926	        else:
927	            if var_name is None:
928	                yield self.text()
929	            else:
930	                yield self.get_var(var_name)
931	
932	    async def text_async_iter(
933	        self, var_name: Optional[str] = None, return_meta_data: bool = False
934	    ):
935	        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
936	
937	        if self.stream_executor.stream:
938	            prev = 0
939	            if var_name is None:
940	                event = self.stream_executor.stream_text_event
941	                while True:
942	                    await loop.run_in_executor(None, event.wait)
943	                    event.clear()
944	                    out = str(self.stream_executor.text_[prev:])
945	                    prev += len(out)
946	                    if out:
947	                        yield out
948	                    if self.stream_executor.is_finished:
949	                        break
950	            else:
951	                event = None
952	                while not event:
953	                    if var_name in self.stream_executor.stream_var_event:
954	                        event = self.stream_executor.stream_var_event[var_name]
955	                    if self.stream_executor.is_finished:
956	                        yield ""
957	                        return
958	
959	                while True:
960	                    await loop.run_in_executor(None, event.wait)
961	                    event.clear()
962	                    out = str(self.stream_executor.variables[var_name][prev:])
963	                    prev += len(out)
964	                    if out:
965	                        if return_meta_data:
966	                            yield out, self.stream_executor.meta_info[var_name]
967	                        else:
968	                            yield out
969	                    if self.stream_executor.variable_event[var_name].is_set():
970	                        break
971	        else:
972	            if var_name is None:
973	                yield self.text()
974	            else:
975	                yield self.get_var(var_name)
976	
977	    def get_var(self, name):
978	        return self.stream_executor.get_var(name)
979	
980	    def set_var(self, name, value):
981	        return self.stream_executor.set_var(name, value)
982	
983	    def get_meta_info(self, name):
984	        return self.stream_executor.get_meta_info(name)
985	
986	    def __iadd__(self, other):
987	        if other is None:
988	            raise ValueError("Tried to append None to state.")
989	        self.stream_executor.submit(other)
990	        return self
991	
992	    def __getitem__(self, name):
993	        return self.get_var(name)
994	
995	    def __setitem__(self, name, value):
996	        self.set_var(name, value)
997	
998	    def __contains__(self, name):
999	        return name in self.stream_executor.variables

75	def gen(
76	    name: Optional[str] = None,
77	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
78	    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
79	    n: Optional[int] = None,
80	    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
81	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
82	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
83	    temperature: Optional[float] = None,
84	    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
85	    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
86	    min_p: Optional[float] = None,
87	    frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
88	    presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
89	    ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
90	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
91	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
92	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
93	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
94	    dtype: Optional[Union[type, str]] = None,
95	    choices: Optional[List[str]] = None,
96	    choices_method: Optional[ChoicesSamplingMethod] = None,
97	    regex: Optional[str] = None,
98	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
99	):
100	    """Call the model to generate. See the meaning of the arguments in docs/backend/sampling_params.md"""
101	
102	    if choices:
103	        return SglSelect(
104	            name,
105	            choices,
106	            0.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
107	            token_length_normalized if choices_method is None else choices_method,
108	        )
109	
110	    # check regex is valid
111	    if regex is not None:
112	        try:
113	            re.compile(regex)
114	        except re.error as e:
115	            raise e
116	
117	    return SglGen(
118	        name,
119	        max_tokens,
120	        min_tokens,
121	        n,
122	        stop,
123	        stop_token_ids,
124	        stop_regex,
125	        temperature,
126	        top_p,
127	        top_k,
128	        min_p,
129	        frequency_penalty,
130	        presence_penalty,
131	        ignore_eos,
132	        return_logprob,
133	        logprob_start_len,
134	        top_logprobs_num,
135	        return_text_in_logprobs,
136	        dtype,
137	        regex,
138	        json_schema,
139	    )

144	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
145	    n: Optional[int] = None,
146	    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
147	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,

150	    top_p: Optional[float] = None,
151	    top_k: Optional[int] = None,
152	    min_p: Optional[float] = None,

7	
8	class TestJSONConstrainedMixin:
9	    json_schema = json.dumps(
10	        {
11	            "type": "object",
12	            "properties": {
13	                "name": {"type": "string", "pattern": "^[\\w]+$"},
14	                "population": {"type": "integer"},
15	            },
16	            "required": ["name", "population"],
17	            "additionalProperties": False,
18	        }
19	    )
20	
21	    def _run_decode_json(
22	        self, json_schema, return_logprob=False, top_logprobs_num=0, n=1
23	    ):
24	        response = requests.post(
25	            self.base_url + "/generate",
26	            json={
27	                "text": (
28	                    "Introduce the capital of France. Return in a JSON format. The JSON Schema is: "
29	                    + json.dumps(json_schema)
30	                ),
31	                "sampling_params": {
32	                    "temperature": 0 if n == 1 else 0.5,
33	                    "max_new_tokens": 128,
34	                    "n": n,
35	                    "stop_token_ids": [119690],
36	                    "json_schema": json_schema,
37	                },
38	                "stream": False,
39	                "return_logprob": return_logprob,
40	                "top_logprobs_num": top_logprobs_num,
41	                "logprob_start_len": 0,
42	            },
43	        )
44	        ret = response.json()
45	        print(json.dumps(ret))
46	        print("=" * 100)
47	
48	        if not json_schema or json_schema == "INVALID":
49	            return
50	
51	        # Make sure the json output is valid
52	        try:
53	            js_obj = json.loads(ret["text"])
54	        except (TypeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError):
55	            raise
56	
57	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["name"], str)
58	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["population"], int)
59	
60	    def test_json_generate(self):
61	        self._run_decode_json(json_schema=self.json_schema)
62	
63	    def test_json_invalid(self):
64	        self._run_decode_json(json_schema="INVALID")
65	
66	    def test_json_openai(self):
67	        client = openai.Client(api_key="EMPTY", base_url=f"{self.base_url}/v1")
68	
69	        response = client.chat.completions.create(
70	            model=self.model,
71	            messages=[
72	                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful AI assistant"},
73	                {
74	                    "role": "user",
75	                    "content": "Introduce the capital of France. Return in a JSON format. "
76	                    "The JSON Schema is: " + json.dumps(self.json_schema),
77	                },
78	            ],
79	            temperature=0,
80	            max_tokens=128,
81	            response_format={
82	                "type": "json_schema",
83	                "json_schema": {"name": "foo", "schema": json.loads(self.json_schema)},
84	            },
85	        )
86	        text = response.choices[0].message.content
87	
88	        try:
89	            js_obj = json.loads(text)
90	        except (TypeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError):
91	            print("JSONDecodeError", text)
92	            raise
93	
94	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["name"], str)
95	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["population"], int)
96	
97	    def test_mix_json_and_other(self):
98	        json_schemas = [None, None, self.json_schema, self.json_schema] * 10
99	
100	        with ThreadPoolExecutor(len(json_schemas)) as executor:
101	            list(executor.map(self._run_decode_json, json_schemas))

3	import logging
4	import time

6	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List
7	
8	import torch

10	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
11	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
12	    create_grammar_backend,
13	)
14	from sglang.srt.environ import envs

16	if TYPE_CHECKING:
17	    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
18	    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
19	    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler

24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)
150	
151	        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
152	        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
153	            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
154	            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
155	        else:
156	            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
157	            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
158	                all_gather_output,
159	                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
160	                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
161	            )
162	            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
163	            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])
164	
165	        # Return ready requests
166	        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
167	        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
168	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
169	            return_reqs.append(req)
170	            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
171	                continue
172	
173	            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
174	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
175	            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
176	                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
177	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
178	
179	        # Return failed requests
180	        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
181	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
182	            return_reqs.append(req)
183	
184	            req.grammar.cancel()
185	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
186	            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
187	            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
188	
189	        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
190	        self.grammar_queue = [
191	            req
192	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
193	            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
194	        ]
195	        return return_reqs

52	    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
53	        """
54	        Accept a token in the grammar.
55	        """
56	        raise NotImplementedError()

58	    def rollback(self, k: int):
59	        raise NotImplementedError()

61	    def is_terminated(self):
62	        return False

64	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
65	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
66	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
67	        raise NotImplementedError()

69	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
70	        raise NotImplementedError()

72	    @staticmethod
73	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
74	        raise NotImplementedError()
75	
76	    @staticmethod
77	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
78	        raise NotImplementedError()
79	
80	    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject":
81	        return self
82	
83	    @property
84	    def finished(self):
85	        return self._finished
86	
87	    @finished.setter

89	        self._finished = finished

91	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
92	        """
93	        Try to jump forward in the grammar.
94	
95	        Returns:
96	            A jump forward helper which may be used in `jump_forward_str_state`.
97	            None if the jump forward is not possible.
98	        """
99	        raise NotImplementedError()

101	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
102	        """
103	        Jump forward for the grammar.
104	
105	        Returns:
106	            A tuple of the jump forward string and the next state of the grammar
107	            (which can be used in `jump_and_retokenize` if needed).
108	        """

111	    def jump_and_retokenize(
112	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
113	    ) -> None:

116	        """

120	INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ = BaseGrammarObject()
121	
122	
123	@dataclass
124	class CacheEntry:
125	    value: BaseGrammarObject
126	    event: Event
127	
128	
129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197	
198	
199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend

14	"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""
15	
16	import logging
17	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union
18	
19	# sre_parse is deprecated in Python 3.11+, use re._parser instead
20	try:
21	    import re._parser as sre_parse
22	except ImportError:
23	    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11
24	
25	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
26	TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30
27	
28	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
29	
30	
31	class SamplingParams:
32	    """
33	    The sampling parameters.
34	
35	    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
36	    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
37	    for the documentation.
38	    """
39	
40	    def __init__(
41	        self,
42	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
43	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
44	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
45	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
46	        temperature: float = 1.0,
47	        top_p: float = 1.0,
48	        top_k: int = -1,
49	        min_p: float = 0.0,
50	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
51	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
52	        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
53	        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
54	        n: int = 1,
55	        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
56	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
57	        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
58	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
59	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
60	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
61	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
62	        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
63	        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
64	        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
65	        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
66	        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
67	    ) -> None:
68	        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
69	        self.stop_strs = stop
70	        if stop_token_ids:
71	            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
72	        else:
73	            self.stop_token_ids = None
74	        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
75	        self.temperature = temperature
76	        self.top_p = top_p
77	        self.top_k = top_k
78	        self.min_p = min_p
79	        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
80	        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
81	        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
82	        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
83	        self.regex = regex
84	        self.n = n
85	        self.json_schema = json_schema
86	        self.ebnf = ebnf
87	        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
88	        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
89	        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
90	        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
91	        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
92	        self.custom_params = custom_params
93	        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
94	        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
95	        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed
96	
97	        # Process some special cases
98	        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
99	            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
100	            self.temperature = 1.0
101	            self.top_k = 1
102	        if self.top_k == -1:
103	            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary
104	
105	    def verify(self, vocab_size):
106	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
107	            raise ValueError(
108	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
109	            )
110	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
111	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
112	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
113	            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
114	        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
115	            raise ValueError(
116	                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
117	            )
118	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
119	            raise ValueError(
120	                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
121	                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
122	            )
123	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
124	            raise ValueError(
125	                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
126	            )
127	        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
128	            raise ValueError(
129	                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
130	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
131	            )
132	        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
133	            raise ValueError(
134	                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
135	                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
136	            )
137	        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
138	            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
139	                raise ValueError(
140	                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
141	                )
142	            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
143	                raise ValueError(
144	                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
145	                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
146	                )
147	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
148	            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
149	                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
150	                    raise ValueError(
151	                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
152	                        f"{token_id}."
153	                    )
154	
155	        grammars = [
156	            self.json_schema,
157	            self.regex,
158	            self.ebnf,
159	        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
160	        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
161	            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
162	
163	    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
164	        # Process stop strings
165	        if self.stop_strs is None:
166	            self.stop_strs = []
167	            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
168	        else:
169	            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
170	                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]
171	
172	            stop_str_max_len = 0
173	            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
174	                if tokenizer is not None:
175	                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
176	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
177	                else:
178	                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
179	            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len
180	
181	        # Process stop regex strings
182	        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
183	            self.stop_regex_strs = []
184	            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
185	        else:
186	            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
187	                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]
188	
189	            stop_regex_max_len = 0
190	            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
191	                stop_regex_max_len = max(
192	                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
193	                )
194	
195	            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len
196	
197

199	# to be buffered to match the input regex string
200	# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
